topic: Implement Fabric v2.0 MCP-First Fortified — tooling, scaffolding, doc-quality scanning, drift detection

findings.finding: MCP TypeScript SDK v1.29.0 (2026-03-30) is production-ready; v2 pre-alpha on main not yet published to latest tag
findings.detail: Pin `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@^1`. Requires zod@3 (v4 has breaking changes), tsconfig module:Node16, package.json type:module, deep imports from /server/mcp.js and /server/stdio.js

findings.finding: All 6 target clients verified MCP-capable in 2026-04 — including Codex
findings.detail: Codex is NOT a fallback, it supports stdio MCP. Codex uses TOML config at ~/.codex/config.toml (unique); all others use JSON with mcpServers key

findings.finding: Codex TOML config divergence requires bi-format sync
findings.detail: fab sync-config must write TOML for Codex, JSON for 5 others. Use smol-toml or @iarna/toml — not hand-rolled serialization

findings.finding: Stdio is the correct transport — HTTP has attack surface + no latency benefit for local tools
findings.detail: MCP spec states clients SHOULD support stdio. Streamable HTTP (new) replaces deprecated SSE. Fabric as local-only tool: stdio exclusively for v1

findings.finding: Never write to stdout in stdio MCP server — any rogue stdout write corrupts JSON-RPC stream
findings.detail: Use process.stderr for all logs. Audit transitive dep logging. This is a silent failure mode

findings.finding: Roo Code docs unreachable (404) — need runtime config path probe
findings.detail: Roo Code (formerly Roo Cline, a Cline fork) MCP config likely in VS Code extension storage: ~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage/rooveterinaryinc.roo-cline/settings/cline_mcp_settings.json. Fabric should probe rather than hardcode

findings.finding: Claude Code has TWO config paths — CLI vs Desktop
findings.detail: CLI: ~/.claude/settings.json. Desktop: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json. Both may need writing depending on user setup

findings.finding: citty (UnJS, v0.2.2 April 2026) is the recommended CLI framework
findings.detail: TS-first, lazy subcommands, tiny, actively maintained. Bundle with `bun build --compile` for single binary. Commander.js is JS-first; clipanion is overkill for 4-6 subcommands

findings.finding: lefthook for Fabric repo itself; recommend husky in generated pre-commit for target projects
findings.detail: Fabric is multi-artifact (CLI + server), lefthook handles parallel hooks. Target projects: husky is familiar (1.5M+ projects, 2kB)

findings.finding: Doc-code drift detection is a novel capability — no mainstream tool does it in 2026
findings.detail: Closest analog: docstring-coverage (Python). Fabric must define its own heuristic — git log --follow comparing code commit count vs AGENTS.md mtime. Pattern: N code files changed but AGENTS.md untouched in 30 days → warn

findings.finding: Projen/Nx pattern for fab init: explicit type inference via package.json + framework config files
findings.detail: Industry does NOT do magic auto-detect. Chain: package.json deps → framework config presence (vite/next/cargo/pyproject) → README quality check (<200 words = stub) → generate AGENTS.md with TODO markers rather than AI-guessing

best_practices.rationale: SDK v2 pre-alpha unstable; Zod v4 has breaking .describe()/z.object() changes SDK not ready for
Current stable API; server.tool() is v2 alias may not work in v1
Required for deep imports from SDK; Bundler resolution breaks

best_practices.practice: Test with MCP Inspector before client integration
best_practices.rationale: `npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node dist/index.js` gives interactive tool testing without real client

best_practices.practice: fab init generates TODO-marker scaffolds, never AI-guesses content without opt-in
best_practices.rationale: Nx generator pattern — humans review scaffold before committing; avoids hallucination blast radius

alternatives.cons: JS-first TS types, no lazy loading, verbose subcommand API
DNS rebinding attack surface, session mgmt overhead, no benefit for local tool
alternatives.verdict: Avoid for v1; reconsider only if Kill Switch 2 (stdio >2s) triggers

pitfalls.issue: Transitive deps writing to stdout silently corrupts stdio MCP stream
pitfalls.mitigation: Audit all deps for stdout; redirect via process.stderr wrapper; use MCP Inspector to catch during dev

pitfalls.issue: Codex TOML vs others JSON — bi-format maintenance
pitfalls.mitigation: Abstract ClientConfigWriter with JSON/TOML implementations; use @iarna/toml not hand-roll

pitfalls.issue: Zod v4 breaks MCP tool registration
pitfalls.mitigation: Lock zod@^3 until SDK declares v4 support

pitfalls.issue: Roo Code config path unverified — may break Day 2 6-client test
pitfalls.mitigation: Runtime probe + fallback to user-supplied path; add to Kill Switch 3 scope

pitfalls.issue: Claude Code Desktop vs CLI config split — Fabric must write both
pitfalls.mitigation: Detect both; offer user confirmation in fab sync-config

codebase_gaps.gap: No MCP SDK version pinning strategy in brainstorm
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Pin SDK v1.29.x, zod v3.x in package.json with rationale comment

codebase_gaps.gap: No explicit plan for Codex TOML divergence in 7-day MVP
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Day 2 says '6 客户端 MCP 配置文件模板' without format distinction
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Day 2 split into 2 subtasks: JSON template (5 clients) + TOML template (Codex); write config as first-class Fabric capability

codebase_gaps.current_approach: fab init 'scan README/CONTRIBUTING, extract technical sections'
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Explicit heuristic chain: parse package.json deps → framework config detection → README quality threshold (200 words, standard sections) → scaffold with TODO markers instead of AI-guess; add `fab scan` as separate read-only diagnosis command

codebase_gaps.gap: Doc drift detection not in MVP
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Add as v1.1 milestone post-MVP: fab drift-check using git log heuristic; keep out of MVP scope

sources.title: MCP TypeScript SDK
sources.key_takeaway: v1.29.0 stable; v2 pre-alpha; use v1.x

sources.title: MCP Transports Spec
sources.key_takeaway: Stdio primary; HTTP for remote only

_metadata.queries_executed: MCP SDK 2026 + 6-client config research + CLI framework comparison + fab init scanning pattern + drift detection landscape
